name: mockup-fidelity
description: Validate that an implemented React or React Native UI faithfully reproduces a reference mockup (HTML+CSS, served prototype, Figma export, or another rendered component set), then update the code to close every gap — by measuring the rendered tree, never by eyeballing or reading source. Treats the mock as the source of truth, builds a COMPLETE screen/frame inventory (never silently drops a frame), extracts both sides to on-disk artifacts and generates the ledger from them, inverts the burden of proof (a visible difference is a defect until a citation proves it intentional), diffs structure-first then per-property computed styles, and produces a per-screen present/divergent/absent ledger plus a FUNCTIONAL-GAPS document for any newly-added UI that is only visual (missing backend, data wiring, endpoints). For React Native it measures the RENDERED tree — structural/accessibility extraction (axe describe-ui / Maestro hierarchy) plus resolved style props over the Metro CDP connection (rozenite / agent-cdp) — and forces every screen's measurement to disk as artifacts, so a 'match' verdict can never come from a screenshot, a source read, or reasoning (frontier vision models catch under half of fine-grained UI differences; an artifact-forcing gate plus a completeness-critic close that hole). For a React web target the comparison is symmetric DOM-to-DOM — reference and target are both read via getComputedStyle through one extractor at the same viewport, with the app chrome (sidebar/header/nav) in scope. Asks the user when unsure, on both a full-comparison and a per-screen basis. Generic and framework-agnostic — nothing app-specific.
